discovered.name: matthisholleville/argocd-mcp

discovered.notes: Low star count but novel design (~200 token system prompt, no hardcoded handlers). Useful blueprint even if not adopted directly; Dex/OAuth model is a good fit for SHA-256 evidence sealing audit chains.

discovered.name: argoproj-labs/argocd-agent

discovered.name: argoproj-labs/argocd-image-updater

discovered.name: alexei-led/k8s-mcp-server

discovered.name: xchangeee/claudernetes

discovered.name: devopssessionsjvr/agentic-ai-demo

discovered.notes: Demo-quality but well-engineered. Mine the MTTR dashboard + auto-fix PR pattern for opsbench evidence sealing on remediation actions.

discovered.name: argocd CLI (argoproj/argo-cd)
discovered.fit_reason: The canonical argocd CLI is the lowest-common-denominator surface for opsbench. Wrap via CLI-Anything for an mcp recipe that works in any environment, including air-gapped/AKS forensic scenarios where MCP servers can't be deployed.
discovered.notes: Stars/pushed not re-fetched due to rate limit — well-known 19k+ star canonical project. Use as the universal fallback for opsbench when MCP isn't available.

trends: ArgoCD MCP is a crowded, fast-moving space in 2026: argoproj-labs/mcp-for-argocd has emerged as the de facto official server (481 stars, Apache-2.0, vendor-blessed), but at least 6 competing implementations exist (Akuity, SeatGeek, severity1, matthisholleville, denysvitali, LokiMCPUniverse) — with the most innovative reading ArgoCD's OpenAPI spec at startup and exposing it via just 2 meta-tools instead of hardcoded handlers. The Argo ecosystem is simultaneously pivoting toward distributed multi-cluster control planes (argocd-agent, pre-GA) and policy-gated promotion (Akuity Kargo's March 2026 Custom Steps), both of which mirror opsbench's Cedar-gated, evidence-sealed action model. AI-authored manifests via ArgoCD CMPs (claudernetes) and AI-driven canary rollback (agentic-ai-demo) are emerging patterns.
gaps: No ArgoCD MCP server today bakes in policy-gated mutating actions with cryptographic evidence sealing — they all expose sync/rollback as plain tool calls trusting the model. Opsbench could lead by shipping a hardened MCP recipe that pairs argoproj-labs/mcp-for-argocd (or seatgeek/argocd-mcp) read-only with Cedar-policy gates and SHA-256-sealed promotion evidence written back via Kargo Custom Steps. There is also no published Claude Code skill that codifies the full GitOps incident-response runbook (app drift -> diff -> guarded rollback -> evidence bundle) — opsbench's team-incident-response is the natural home for this.

discovered.name: docker/mcp-gateway
discovered.fit_reason: Official Docker CLI plugin that manages and routes MCP servers as isolated containers with secret/OAuth handling. opsbench team-incident-response and a future team-platform-engineering can run vetted MCP servers behind a single gateway and apply per-agent tool allowlists upstream of Cedar policy.
discovered.notes: Pairs natively with Docker Desktop's MCP Toolkit; gives container-level isolation for MCP servers which complements opsbench evidence-sealing model.

discovered.name: docker/mcp-registry
discovered.fit_reason: Official curated catalog of 300+ signed MCP servers with SBOMs, provenance, and Docker-built images. opsbench should source MCP recipes from this registry rather than ad-hoc community images to keep evidence chain clean.
discovered.notes: Provides cryptographic signatures and SBOMs — directly usable for opsbench SHA-256 evidence sealing model.

discovered.name: docker/cagent
discovered.fit_reason: Docker's official YAML-declarative multi-agent runtime with MCP, RAG, hierarchical delegation, and OCI registry packaging. opsbench could express team-incident-response sub-agents as cagent YAML and distribute them as signed OCI artifacts, matching its team-as-container concept.
discovered.notes: Provider-agnostic (Anthropic/OpenAI/Gemini/Bedrock/DMR). Has docker/cagent-action for GitHub Actions integration.

discovered.name: ckreiling/mcp-server-docker
discovered.fit_reason: Mature MCP server exposing full Docker CRUD (containers, images, networks, volumes, logs, stats). Drop-in tool surface for an opsbench team-incident-response agent to inspect a misbehaving container or pull logs during forensic IR.
discovered.notes: GPL-3.0 — vendoring source into opsbench would copyleft; safer to run as external MCP server. Author explicitly warns Docker is not a secure sandbox; constrain via Cedar allowlist.

discovered.name: docker/docker-language-server

discovered.notes: Go-based. Pulls Scout CVE data into LSP hovers — useful diagnostic surface for an agent.

discovered.name: compose-spec/compose-go
discovered.fit_reason: Reference Go library for parsing, normalizing, and validating Compose YAML against the spec. opsbench team-platform-engineering can call this to validate AI-generated docker-compose.yml before letting agents apply changes — a deterministic gate identical in spirit to its Cedar policy check.
discovered.notes: Maintained by Docker; ships JSON schema (compose-spec.json) usable from any language.

discovered.name: docker (Docker Scout CLI)
discovered.fit_reason: Built-in CLI for image SBOM, CVE matching against 23 advisory sources, and policy evaluation. team-incident-response can shell `docker scout cves <image>` during a forensic sweep to attach CVE evidence to the sealed bundle.
discovered.notes: Closed-source CLI bundled with Docker Desktop 4.17+. Free tier has rate limits; supports SPDX/CycloneDX output for evidence-friendly formats.

discovered.name: aquasecurity/trivy
discovered.fit_reason: Apache-licensed, FOSS alternative/complement to Docker Scout: scans images, filesystems, IaC, secrets, K8s. Critical for opsbench's existing K8s/AKS forensic pipeline — already aligned with incident response evidence needs.
discovered.notes: JSON/SARIF/CycloneDX output formats; deterministic exit codes make it a clean tool for Cedar-gated execution.

discovered.name: docker/buildx

discovered.name: wagoodman/dive

discovered.name: QuantGeekDev/docker-mcp

discovered.name: elusznik/mcp-server-code-execution-mode

discovered.name: ajeetraina/docker-mcp-toolkit
discovered.fit_reason: Community-curated index of the Docker MCP Catalog with usage notes. Good cross-reference when picking which MCPs to wire into opsbench team allowlists.
discovered.notes: Curated list, not a tool. Useful for discovery only.

discovered.name: docker/cagent-action
discovered.fit_reason: Official GitHub Action that runs cagent agents in CI workflows. Pattern for how opsbench could ship team agents as composable GitHub Actions for CI-triggered IR (e.g., auto-respond to a security alert PR).

trends: Docker has fully embraced MCP in 2025-2026: the official Docker MCP Catalog/Toolkit (300+ signed servers with SBOMs and provenance), docker/mcp-gateway as a routing/auth control plane, and docker/cagent as a YAML-declarative multi-agent runtime distributed via OCI registries are now the canonical stack. Container-isolated sandboxes for AI agents (microsandbox, agent-infra/sandbox, code-sandbox-mcp) are exploding — agent execution is converging on "one container per agent" with rootless and microVM variants. Docker is also pulling deterministic dev-time tooling (LSP for Dockerfile/Compose/Bake with Scout CVE hovers, compose-go as the parser) into the AI loop so agents stop guessing YAML.
gaps: No opinionated incident-response MCP recipe exists that combines Docker Scout/Trivy scanning, container log capture, dive-style layer diff, and SHA-256 evidence sealing into a single Cedar-gated workflow — opsbench is well-placed to ship this. The Docker MCP catalog is breadth-optimized (300+ servers) but lacks curated, narrow team profiles for SRE/IR/Platform/Security personas with pre-tuned allowlists; opsbench's "team-*" packaging fills that void. There is also no widely-adopted pattern for chaining compose-go validation -> buildx provenance -> Scout policy -> deployment as a single agent-callable platform-engineering pipeline with deterministic gates between each stage.

trends: The Ansible ecosystem has pivoted hard toward MCP in the last 12 months: Red Hat shipped an official Ansible Development Tools MCP server (npm + container) and an AAP 2.6.4 tech-preview MCP server, while a tier of community MCP servers (sibilleb, rlopez133, washyu, mancubus77, kpeacocke) covers both AAP/AWX and Event-Driven Ansible. In parallel a Claude Code "skills" subculture has formed — hello-ansible-skills (62 stars), claude-ansible-skills, 3A2DEV/ansible-designer, olandodeflexy/ansible-skill — all converging on Red Hat CoP good-practice rules and diagnose-first workflows. The strategic narrative from Red Hat is explicit: Ansible is repositioning as the trusted execution layer for agentic AI rather than just an authoring target.
gaps: No MCP server we found exposes EDA + AAP + ansible-lint + evidence-sealing as a single forensic-grade tool surface — every server picks one slice, leaving an opsbench-shaped hole for a Cedar-gated, SHA-256-evidence-sealed Ansible MCP recipe that mirrors the existing K8s/AKS one. There is also no public skill that ties Ansible remediation to incident-response evidence chains (capture -> classify -> ansible-playbook-apply with sealed proof of what changed), and no GPL-clean opsbench-style template for hardened Execution Environments containing only allowlisted modules. Finally, the ecosystem lacks a cross-vendor pattern for delegating from a K8s forensic agent to an Ansible remediation agent with policy continuity — a natural team-incident-response x team-platform-engineering bridge for opsbench to ship.

trends: In 2026 the Terraform agent ecosystem has clearly bifurcated: (1) an official MCP layer (hashicorp/terraform-mcp-server, the terraform Claude plugin) is now the canonical way agents reach the Registry and HCP/TFE; (2) a fast-growing skill layer (antonbabenko/terraform-skill, terrashark, terramate agent-skills, hashi-demo-lab stacks skill) focuses on eliminating LLM hallucinations and enforcing HashiCorp best practices in agent-emitted HCL. Two macro shifts amplify this: HCP Terraform's free-tier cap (Mar 2026) is driving migration to OpenTofu plus self-hosted orchestrators (Terrateam, Terragrunt Scale), and "guardrails between agent and cloud" tools (ops0-cli, tf-dialect) are emerging as a category of their own — mirroring opsbench's Cedar-policy gating.
gaps: The biggest gap is evidence-grade audit: existing Terraform MCP servers/CLIs emit plan and scan output but none seal it with hashes the way opsbench's SHA-256 evidence chain does, so an opsbench team-platform-engineering skill that wraps terraform plan + Checkov + Trivy + Infracost into a single signed evidence bundle would be net-new. Second, no project ties Terraform drift detection to a forensic incident-response timeline (the closest is Terrateam's drift-detect, but it lives in a GitOps PR loop, not an SRE incident graph) — opsbench could fill this by reusing its Kubernetes forensic chain for Terraform state diffs. Third, OpenTofu-first agent skills are essentially absent; most projects still say "Terraform" and treat tofu as an alias, leaving room for a true tofu-native opsbench skill that handles state encryption and module registries differently.

trends: As of mid-2026 the Packer ecosystem is consolidating around two patterns: (1) HashiCorp's official Agent Skills package (650 stars, weekly commits) is becoming the canonical way agents learn Packer/HCP semantics, and (2) HashiCorp ships an official Terraform MCP server but has not yet released a Packer MCP equivalent, leaving a small but visible gap that community projects (warpgate-mcp-server) are racing to fill. Security-first image builds are the dominant operational theme - CVE/misconfig scanners as Packer provisioners (cnspec, Trivy, Qualys) and SLSA/cosign-signed image attestations are now expected in golden-image pipelines, especially in GCP and AKS shops.
gaps: There is no official HashiCorp Packer MCP server, and no MCP recipe yet exposes HCP Packer's channel-promotion, bucket, and run-task APIs to agents - opsbench could ship the first credible one by mirroring terraform-mcp-server's Go/MPL-2.0 architecture and adding Cedar policies for promote/delete actions. Second gap: no agent-native bridge exists between Packer build output and forensic/IR evidence (no MCP tool that records SHA-256 manifests of built AMIs or signs them with cosign keyed to the IR case), which is exactly opsbench's existing evidence-sealing primitive. Third gap: nobody is wrapping vulnerability scanners (cnspec/trivy/grype) as standalone Packer-provisioner MCP tools with per-agent allowlists, so opsbench has clear room to build a 'secure-image-build' recipe family that the broader DevOps audience would adopt.

trends: Vault MCP coverage went from zero to an official HashiCorp-maintained server (hashicorp/vault-mcp-server, MPL-2.0, actively pushed June 2026) plus a long tail of MIT/AGPL community variants in the last six months, and most of them deliberately constrain themselves to KV + policy + PKI (leaving Transit/AppRole/dynamic-secrets to CLI). On the Kubernetes side, the dominant 2026 pattern is declarative reconciliation — hashicorp/vault-secrets-operator and external-secrets/external-secrets are both at active 2026 release cadence — and tokenization/credential-broker patterns ('agent never sees the raw secret', e.g. theagentattic/mcp-vault) are emerging in direct response to early-2026 ToxicSkills-style supply-chain attacks. CNCF-backed CLI tooling (bank-vaults v1.33.1 in May 2026) remains the safe fallback for unseal/rekey/HSM operations that no MCP server covers yet.
gaps: No MCP server today exposes the full Transit, AppRole, dynamic-database-secrets, or unseal/rekey surface — opsbench could ship a 'vault-ops-extended' MCP recipe that wraps bank-vaults + soerenschneider/vault-pki-cli to fill these holes. There is also no opinionated Vault skill for incident response (e.g. 'rotate all credentials touched by compromised pod X, seal evidence, file Cedar-approved change'), which is squarely opsbench's existing K8s/AKS forensic muscle and would be a high-leverage skill to author. Finally, the ecosystem lacks a credential-broker pattern that combines Cedar-style policy gating with Vault response-wrapping — opsbench's per-agent tool allowlist + SHA-256 evidence sealing is uniquely positioned to publish a reference implementation here.

trends: Crossplane v2 + Upbound 2.0 went 'AI-native' in early 2026 with vendor-published MCP servers (marketplace + control plane) and IDE/CLI tooling that explicitly markets Claude/OpenAI integration, and the wider ecosystem is converging on composition functions written in real languages (KCL, Python, CEL/kro) rather than patch-and-transform YAML — both moves make Crossplane unusually agent-friendly compared to Terraform. On the operator side, CNCF kagent (2.9k stars, Istio founders) is establishing the 'agents as CRDs with MCP tool servers' pattern that other platforms are copying, and Crossplane is showing up inside those reference stacks (shilucloud/crossplane-agent). Read-only troubleshooting MCP servers (briferz) and BDD test frameworks (Swisscom) are appearing specifically to support agent workflows that must prove safety before applying changes.

There is also no Cedar-gated (or any policy-gated) write path for Crossplane operations; existing MCP servers either expose full CRUD or nothing, so opsbench could ship the first 'safe write' Crossplane MCP that gates `kubectl apply` of XRs/claims through Cedar with dry-run preview and PR-mode output. discovered.name: agentgateway/agentgateway
discovered.fit_reason: Rust-on-Envoy AI-native data plane that natively speaks MCP, A2A, and OpenAI APIs. opsbench team-incident-response and a future team-platform-engineering can use it to route, authn, rate-limit, and observe MCP traffic from sub-agents through one Linux Foundation-blessed gateway, and the project is the reference target for routing Claude Code traffic out to k8s/Istio/Helm MCP tools.
discovered.notes: v1.x alpha tags (1.3.0-alpha) — still pre-GA but already in CNCF/Linux Foundation orbit and the recommended fronting proxy for Claude Code → MCP per agentgateway docs. Best paired with kgateway control plane.

discovered.name: kagent-dev/kagent
discovered.fit_reason: CNCF-sandbox Kubernetes-native agent runtime with CRDs for Agent/ModelConfig/ToolServers and prebuilt MCP tool integrations for Istio, Helm, Argo, and observability. opsbench can model team-platform-engineering as kagent Agents and reuse its toolserver pattern (Cedar-style policy + per-agent allowlists map cleanly) rather than reinventing a runtime.
discovered.notes: 144 releases, 300+ contributors incl. MS/AWS/Oracle. Heavy footprint — adopt as architectural blueprint or selectively vendor the istio/helm tool packages instead of running the full runtime in cluster.

discovered.name: kagent-dev/tools
discovered.fit_reason: Single Go MCP server exposing tool suites for Kubernetes, Helm, Istio, Cilium, Prometheus, Grafana, and Argo Rollouts — exactly the surface team-incident-response needs to triage ingress/mesh-layer incidents (e.g., '500s spiking on this Gateway, find the misconfigured VirtualService'). Drop-in MCP recipe candidate alongside opsbench's existing AKS forensic flow.
discovered.notes: Low stars but maintained by the kagent core team; recently migrated Python→Go for smaller footprint. Pair with Cedar gating in opsbench since these tools include mutating actions (scale, patch).

discovered.name: kgateway-dev/kgateway
discovered.fit_reason: CNCF Envoy-based Gateway API implementation that explicitly serves as the control plane for agentgateway and supports inference/MCP routing. opsbench platform-engineering skills can target kgateway CRDs (Gateway, HTTPRoute, AI policies) as the canonical surface for any 'fix the ingress' or 'rollout a new route' workflow on agent-aware clusters.
discovered.notes: Stable GA (v2.3.1). Originated as Gloo. opsbench would author skills that operate via kubectl/Helm against kgateway CRDs, not vendor the controller itself.

discovered.name: envoyproxy/ai-gateway
discovered.fit_reason: Two-tier Envoy Gateway extension that brokers requests to 16+ LLM providers with rate-limiting and inference picker support. Useful reference architecture for opsbench when team-platform-engineering is asked to stand up an LLM egress gateway, and a likely policy target for team-security skills auditing AI traffic.
discovered.notes: Less MCP-focused than agentgateway but is the Envoy project's official line. Good source of CRD/Policy examples to encode as opsbench remediation skills.

discovered.name: krutsko/istio-mcp-server
discovered.fit_reason: Read-only MCP server exposing Istio VirtualServices, DestinationRules, Gateways, and live Envoy proxy config. Perfect low-risk addition for opsbench team-incident-response when triaging mesh routing or TLS issues — read-only fits opsbench's allowlist model with zero Cedar policy complexity.
discovered.notes: Very low stars and last release Sep 2025 — borderline stale, but the 100% read-only design and STDIO/SSE/HTTP transports make it trivially safe to wire as an MCP recipe. Consider vendoring or forking if upstream goes dark.

discovered.name: ffelicissimo/servicemesh-mcp-server
discovered.fit_reason: JavaScript MCP server with 46+ tools across the Istio API surface plus Kubernetes Gateway API, including mutating actions (VirtualService/DestinationRule/AuthorizationPolicy/Telemetry). Adds the write-side counterpart to istio-mcp-server for opsbench remediation skills, gated behind Cedar policies.
discovered.notes: Zero stars and one-commit history — treat as a reference for tool surface and reimplement under opsbench rather than depend on it. Targets OpenShift Service Mesh 3.x and Istio 1.20+.

discovered.name: alibaba/higress
discovered.fit_reason: Istio+Envoy cloud-native API gateway with first-class MCP server hosting, openapi-to-mcp converter, and a documented nginx-to-higress AI migration skill — the migration pattern is directly portable to an opsbench 'migrate-ingress-nginx' skill, and Higress's Wasm plugin model is a useful blueprint for policy-enforced agent egress.
discovered.notes: Large project with broad ingress-nginx replacement narrative. Adopt the migration-skill pattern (CNCF blog 2026/04/23) rather than the runtime unless an opsbench user already runs Higress.

discovered.name: containers/kubernetes-mcp-server
discovered.fit_reason: Native-Go Kubernetes/OpenShift MCP server that does CRUD on any resource (so it can list/edit Ingress, HTTPRoute, Gateway, VirtualService), plus Helm and Kiali service-mesh tools. Strongest general-purpose MCP backbone for opsbench team-incident-response — covers ingress/gateway out of the box via generic resource ops.
discovered.notes: Direct K8s API client (not kubectl wrapper) so latency and audit trails are clean. Pair tightly with Cedar gating since CRUD covers mutating actions across the whole cluster.

discovered.name: katanemo/archgw
discovered.fit_reason: Rust-on-Envoy 'intelligent prompt gateway' with jailbreak protection, moderation filter chains, and unified LLM routing. Useful blueprint for opsbench team-security or future team-llm-ops skills tasked with standing up safe agent egress; HN-validated (118 + 31 points).
discovered.notes: Now branded 'Plano' / archgw. Strong overlap with agentgateway — pick one as the canonical AI gateway opsbench skills target; archgw wins on guardrails/moderation maturity, agentgateway wins on MCP/A2A depth.

discovered.name: kagent-dev/kmcp
discovered.fit_reason: Toolkit for taking MCP servers from prototype to production on Kubernetes with native CRDs, FastMCP/Go SDK scaffolding, and integrated secrets management. Useful when opsbench needs to package its own MCP recipes (e.g., a custom 'aks-forensics' MCP) for in-cluster deployment with proper allowlists and TLS.
discovered.notes: Solid blueprint for the 'opsbench-MCPs-as-a-Kubernetes-platform' direction. Not needed if opsbench keeps MCPs stdio-local to Claude Code.

discovered.name: traefik (Traefik Hub MCP/AI controls)
discovered.fit_reason: Traefik Proxy 3.7 + Hub 3.20 add protocol-level MCP protection, token-cost controls, structured refusals, and multi-cluster API federation as the GA Ingress-NGINX replacement. Valuable reference architecture (especially given Ingress-NGINX EOL) for an opsbench 'audit MCP egress through Traefik' skill or 'migrate-off-ingress-nginx' runbook.
discovered.notes: Hub is commercial; Traefik Proxy itself is MIT/Apache. Treat as a documented pattern + CLI target (`traefik` / `traefik hub` CLI) rather than an MCP integration upstream.

discovered.name: kguardian-dev/kguardian
discovered.fit_reason: eBPF-based Kubernetes security profile generator with a built-in MCP server speaking to Claude/OpenAI/Gemini/Copilot for natural-language queries about cluster traffic and policies. Slots beside opsbench's AKS forensics recipe to add ingress/east-west traffic introspection at the eBPF layer.
discovered.notes: Early-stage (HN Show 2026). Validate licensing before vendoring; the eBPF angle complements Cilium-based clusters that opsbench is likely to encounter.

discovered.name: Kubernetes AI Gateway Working Group
discovered.fit_reason: Upstream WG defining the Gateway API + Inference Extension surface (envoy-gateway, kgateway, GKE Gateway) for AI workloads. Authoritative spec source for any opsbench skill that creates/edits AI Gateway resources — use to keep the team-platform-engineering CRD library aligned with upstream standards.
discovered.notes: Not a deployable artifact; consume as a living spec. Track the gateway-api-inference-extension SIG repo for CRD changes.

trends: The ingress/gateway space pivoted hard toward 'AI-native gateways' in 2026: Envoy-backed Rust dataplanes (agentgateway, archgw) and Go control planes (kgateway, Envoy AI Gateway) now treat MCP and A2A as first-class protocols alongside HTTP/gRPC, while the Kubernetes AI Gateway WG (March 2026) and the Gateway API Inference Extension formalized the CRD surface. Solo.io's kagent + agentgateway donation to CNCF/Linux Foundation, plus Traefik's GA Ingress-NGINX replacement with MCP/TBAC controls, signal that gateway vendors view agent traffic governance — not pod-to-pod routing — as the new differentiator. Read-only MCP servers for service mesh (istio-mcp-server, kagent tools, kubernetes-mcp-server) are also proliferating as the safe on-ramp for Claude Code-style agents.
gaps: There is no Cedar-policy-gated, evidence-sealing MCP server purpose-built for ingress/gateway forensics (e.g., 'why is this Gateway returning 503', 'diff this HTTPRoute against last known good', SHA-256-sealed Envoy config dumps) — every existing MCP either exposes raw CRUD (kubernetes-mcp-server, servicemesh-mcp-server) or is read-only-broad (istio-mcp-server) with no audit trail. There is also no opinionated 'migrate-off-ingress-nginx' agent skill despite EOL pressure and CNCF-blessed migration paths (Higress, kgateway, Traefik), and nothing wires Cilium/Hubble eBPF flow data into an MCP surface for L4/L7 incident triage. opsbench can fill these by: (1) shipping a gateway-incident-response skill that fronts kagent-dev/tools + istio-mcp-server behind Cedar, (2) a platform-engineering 'rotate-ingress-controller' template-blueprint skill, and (3) a Hubble/Cilium MCP recipe for ingress-layer forensics.

discovered.name: inspektor-gadget/ig-mcp-server
discovered.fit_reason: Official MCP server from Inspektor Gadget (CNCF) exposing eBPF-powered DNS traces, TCP connections, syscalls, and network flow gadgets directly to AI agents. Lets opsbench team-incident-response invoke kernel-level network forensics on AKS/K8s pods without writing eBPF code, perfect for the existing K8s incident response recipe.
discovered.notes: Vendor-published, actively maintained. HolmesGPT already integrates it as of 2026-03; opsbench should mirror that pattern. Requires gadget runtime on cluster nodes (DaemonSet).

discovered.name: kubeshark/kubeshark
discovered.fit_reason: 11.9k-star eBPF K8s traffic analyzer that already ships an MCP interface ("queryable by AI agents via MCP" per upstream description) plus dashboard. Indexes L4/L7 with full K8s context and decrypts TLS without keys, ideal forensic surface for opsbench team-incident-response post-incident packet review.
discovered.notes: Largest community in the eBPF K8s network observability space. Self-tagged with incident-response, sre, mcp topics. Heavy resource footprint; recipe should document node selectors and TTL on captures for evidence sealing.

discovered.name: cilium/pwru
discovered.fit_reason: eBPF-based Linux kernel packet tracer ("packet, where are you?") that prints every kernel function a packet traverses. Wrap via cli-anything for a team-incident-response skill that pinpoints where in the netfilter/conntrack/CNI stack a connection drops, complementing higher-level k8s tools.
discovered.notes: No MCP server upstream. Needs root + BTF-enabled kernel; recipe must lock down via Cedar (cap-restrict to specific nodes / time-boxed). Output is verbose, consider summarization tool.

discovered.name: microsoft/retina
discovered.fit_reason: Microsoft's eBPF distributed network observability stack for AKS/K8s with retina capture (pod-level pcap to blob storage) and traffic metrics. Direct match for opsbench AKS-focused recipes; capture artifacts integrate cleanly with SHA-256 evidence sealing pipeline.
discovered.notes: Microsoft-maintained, AKS-native. No MCP yet (gap opsbench could fill); for now wrap kubectl-retina CLI. Captures land in Azure Storage which fits existing AKS forensic flow.

discovered.name: nicolaka/netshoot
discovered.fit_reason: 10.7k-star debug container with tcpdump, mtr, nmap, iperf, dig, drill, ngrep, conntrack, etc. opsbench team-incident-response should standardize on netshoot as the ephemeralcontainer image for in-cluster network forensics, matching kubectl debug recipes.
discovered.notes: Not an MCP server but the de-facto reference toolbox. Use as the runtime image for any in-cluster shell-out skill; the SHA-pinned digest belongs in the agent allowlist.

discovered.name: 0xKoda/WireMCP
discovered.fit_reason: 489-star MCP server wrapping tshark for real-time packet capture + PCAP analysis exposed as tools to Claude/Cursor. Lets team-incident-response request "summarize the last 60s of traffic on pod X" or analyze a sealed pcap from evidence storage.
discovered.notes: Last push July 2025 - borderline staleness but most-starred tshark MCP in the space. Active forks (mixelpixx, A-G-U-P-T-A) exist if upstream stagnates further. Tool surface needs allowlist gating since live capture is sensitive.

discovered.name: mixelpixx/Wireshark-MCP
discovered.fit_reason: Active production-grade Wireshark MCP with 18 packet-analysis tools and JSON streaming, refreshed Feb 2026. Useful alternative/fallback to WireMCP if opsbench wants a more recently maintained tshark MCP for offline PCAP analysis of sealed evidence.
discovered.notes: Low stars but fresh activity and richer tool surface. Worth tracking in parallel with WireMCP and picking the survivor.

discovered.name: FuzzingLabs/mcp-security-hub
discovered.fit_reason: 567-star bundle of MCP servers for offensive tools including Nmap. Useful template for an opsbench team-security skill that needs read-only nmap port discovery during incident triage (e.g., 'what's listening on this pod's hostNetwork').
discovered.notes: Offensive-security framing - opsbench would need to fork and restrict to passive/discovery scans only. Good multi-tool MCP packaging pattern to copy.

discovered.name: PhialsBasement/nmap-mcp-server
discovered.fit_reason: Focused nmap MCP server, recently active. Drop-in for a team-platform-engineering skill that needs targeted host/service discovery (e.g., verify nodeport reachability during incident) without the offensive-security framing of mcp-security-hub.
discovered.notes: Smaller surface = easier Cedar policy gating. Pair with strict allowlist for scan targets (only cluster CIDRs).

discovered.name: eunomia-bpf/MCPtrace
discovered.fit_reason: MCP server that uses eBPF for kernel tracing. Closest agent-callable analogue to pwru/bpftrace and a useful pattern for opsbench when building a custom kernel-trace skill for deep network stack diagnostics.
discovered.notes: From the eunomia-bpf project (well-known eBPF research group). Use as architecture reference for a Cedar-gated, time-boxed kernel-tracing recipe.

discovered.name: vedevpatel/mcp-network-diagnostics
discovered.fit_reason: Purpose-built MCP server for AI-driven network latency diagnostics using topology + metrics. Good blueprint for an opsbench latency-investigation skill that correlates hops with prometheus/Hubble flow data.
discovered.notes: Tiny project, low stars - use as design blueprint rather than vendored dependency. Apache-2.0 makes copying patterns clean.

discovered.name: tcpiplab/Instability
discovered.fit_reason: Network diagnostic chatbot with 50+ built-in tools (ping, traceroute, dig, http checks, etc.) accessible via MCP and CLI. Works offline. Useful as a fallback / reference for what tool surface opsbench's own network-diag skill should expose.
discovered.notes: No declared license - blocks vendoring. Active development. Mine the tool list for completeness checks against opsbench skills.

discovered.name: cyanheads/toolkit-mcp-server
discovered.fit_reason: Apache-2.0 MCP server bundling IP geolocation, DNS/network diagnostics, system monitoring, and crypto utilities. Cheap drop-in for opsbench team-incident-response when an investigator needs WHOIS / geo / DNS context on an external IP found in flow logs.
discovered.notes: Multi-purpose; if used, allowlist only the network-diag subset of tools so the agent does not get crypto/QR side effects.

discovered.name: marilynceo/netdiag-mcp
discovered.fit_reason: MCP server explicitly covering ping, traceroute, DNS, HTTP, SSL, port scan, MTR, WHOIS, and bandwidth tests with offline-first design. Matches the exact tool list opsbench team-incident-response would shell out to from a netshoot pod.
discovered.notes: Zero stars, unlicensed - use as design template only. Good checklist of which classic net-diag commands deserve MCP wrapping.

discovered.name: eldadru/ksniff
discovered.fit_reason: 3.4k-star kubectl plugin that runs tcpdump inside a target pod and streams to local Wireshark. Wrap via cli-anything to give opsbench team-incident-response one-command pod-level pcap capture that lands directly in SHA-256-sealed evidence storage.
discovered.notes: Last push Aug 2024 - approaching stale but still the canonical kubectl sniffing plugin. No MCP upstream. Verify it works on current k8s before vendoring.

discovered.name: inspektor-gadget/inspektor-gadget
discovered.fit_reason: CNCF eBPF data collection framework underlying ig-mcp-server. Provides kubectl-gadget for trace dns, trace tcp, trace exec, profile cpu. Even without the MCP server, opsbench can call kubectl-gadget directly from a Cedar-gated skill.
discovered.notes: CNCF Sandbox project; security audited (Shielder, April 2026). Use as the runtime backing whether opsbench goes mcp-recipe or cli-wrap path.

trends: eBPF has become the dominant network-diagnostics substrate, with every serious 2026 project (Inspektor Gadget, Kubeshark, Retina, pwru, Cilium/Hubble) using it for zero-config kernel-level visibility. The MCP-server wave hit this domain hard in late 2025 / early 2026: vendor-published servers (Inspektor Gadget's ig-mcp-server, Kubeshark's built-in MCP) now sit alongside community wrappers for tshark (WireMCP, Wireshark-MCP) and nmap, and HolmesGPT's CNCF integration with Inspektor Gadget shows the pattern of LLM-driven incident investigators picking the right eBPF gadget autonomously. The third trend is consolidation - "all-in-one" net-diag MCP servers (toolkit-mcp-server, netdiag-mcp, Instability) bundling ping/traceroute/dig/WHOIS/MTR are emerging as a recognizable archetype.
gaps: Nothing in the ecosystem ties network-diag MCP output to evidence-sealing pipelines - PCAPs from Kubeshark, Retina, or ksniff are not currently hashed, signed, or chain-of-custody tracked, which is exactly opsbench's differentiator with SHA-256 evidence sealing and Cedar gating. There is no Cilium/Hubble-specific MCP server (only generic "eBPF observability" wrappers), so opsbench could publish a first-class hubble-mcp-recipe that exposes flow queries with policy-aware filtering. Finally, AKS-native Retina has no MCP at all yet - building a retina-mcp wrapping retina capture would slot perfectly into opsbench's existing AKS forensic incident-response recipe and currently has no upstream competition.

discovered.name: containers/kubernetes-mcp-server
discovered.fit_reason: Native Go MCP server that talks straight to the Kubernetes API (no kubectl shell-out), with full CRUD, pod exec/logs/top, Helm, KubeVirt, and a read-only mode — exactly the surface opsbench's team-incident-response agent needs across any conformant distro (k3s/RKE2/Talos/kind/EKS).
discovered.notes: Read-only mode + OpenTelemetry tracing pair well with opsbench's evidence-sealing + Cedar gating. Distro-agnostic via API server; gives forensic incident response on any cluster without per-distro work.

discovered.name: Nosmoht/talos-mcp-server
discovered.fit_reason: MCP server speaking Talos's native gRPC apid (not kubectl) so opsbench can inspect dmesg, runtime events, etcd snapshots, machine config, and run pre-upgrade diagnostics on Talos nodes — the layer kubectl-based MCPs cannot reach during a node-level incident.
discovered.notes: Very new (June 2026, 0 stars) but actively released; mutating ops gated behind confirm=true which maps cleanly onto opsbench's Cedar approval flow. Uses existing ~/.talos/config mTLS creds.

discovered.name: ry-ops/k3s-mcp-server
discovered.fit_reason: Lightweight k3s-focused MCP wrapping kubectl for pod/deploy/service/node ops; useful as a per-distro recipe template when opsbench needs a smaller, edge-friendly footprint than the full containers/ server.
discovered.notes: Tiny user base; treat as a blueprint to fork or as reference for the k3s-specific recipe rather than a hard dependency.

discovered.name: devantler-tech/ksail
discovered.fit_reason: Single SDK/CLI that provisions and manages Kind, K3d, Talos, VCluster, KWOK, EKS with built-in GitOps (Flux/Argo), SOPS+age secrets, and an MCP server compatible with Claude Code — ideal backbone for a future team-platform-engineering 'spin up forensic cluster' skill.
discovered.notes: License is PolyForm Shield 1.0.0 (non-OSI) — restricts use in competing products. Fine as a vendored CLI / MCP backend for opsbench skills, but flag clearly in EVIDENCE.md and SBOM.

discovered.name: awslabs/mcp (Amazon EKS MCP Server)
discovered.fit_reason: Official AWS-maintained MCP server for EKS cluster admin, manifest deploys, pod log/event pulling, and CloudFormation-backed provisioning — direct fit for opsbench's existing AKS forensic recipes' AWS counterpart.
discovered.notes: Monorepo also contains CloudWatch, IAM, and other AWS MCP servers — natural cross-reference for opsbench's cloud-side forensic evidence collection.

discovered.name: kagent-dev/kagent
discovered.fit_reason: Kubernetes-native agent runtime (CNCF-aligned) with first-class MCP support, built-in tools for k8s/Istio/Helm/Argo/Prometheus/Grafana/Cilium, and explicit incident-response + platform-engineering use cases — strong reference architecture for opsbench's CRD-driven future.
discovered.notes: Doesn't itself slot into opsbench, but its agent CRDs, MCP tool catalog, and 'pager-aware triage' pattern are worth copying. Cross-reference in design docs.

discovered.name: rohitg00/kubectl-mcp-server
discovered.fit_reason: 253-tool, multi-transport (stdio/SSE/HTTP) kubectl MCP with non-destructive mode, secret masking, RBAC validation, and OAuth 2.1 — useful as a higher-level natural-language layer on top of opsbench's lower-level Cedar-gated tools.
discovered.notes: Tool sprawl (253 tools + 107 ecosystem integrations) clashes with opsbench's tight per-agent allowlist philosophy. Cite as prior art; do not import wholesale.

discovered.name: k0sproject/k0sctl
discovered.fit_reason: Official YAML-driven bootstrap/upgrade/backup/restore CLI for k0s with first-class AGENTS.md guidance — perfect candidate to wrap via CLI-Anything for a team-platform-engineering 'safely upgrade k0s control plane' skill.
discovered.notes: AGENTS.md presence signals upstream is friendly to LLM consumers. No MCP exists yet — opsbench could publish the first.

discovered.name: kubernetes-sigs/kubespray
discovered.fit_reason: Battle-tested Ansible playbooks with explicit add/replace/remove-node plays — opsbench can wrap selected playbooks behind a Cedar-gated 'quarantine-and-replace-node' incident skill rather than reimplement node lifecycle.
discovered.notes: Ansible has a heavy footprint; only wrap a curated subset (cluster.yml, scale.yml, remove-node.yml, upgrade-cluster.yml). Inventory generation belongs outside opsbench.

discovered.name: canonical/microk8s
discovered.fit_reason: Single-binary snap-based distro with rich addons (DNS, storage, observability, GPU) controllable via `microk8s` subcommand — wrap via CLI-Anything for edge/IoT forensic scenarios where Talos/EKS are overkill.
discovered.notes: Snap-only install constrains evidence-gathering hosts. Pair with canonical/microk8s-cluster-agent for HA-cluster ops if opsbench targets edge fleets.

discovered.name: siderolabs/talos
discovered.fit_reason: Reference immutable-OS distro itself; talosctl is the canonical CLI to wrap (or back via Nosmoht's MCP) for opsbench forensic actions on Talos clusters — node reboot, dmesg, etcd snapshot, machine config diff/patch with dry-run.
discovered.notes: MPL-2.0 is permissive enough for wrapping but copyleft on modified files. Pair with the talos MCP recipe rather than vendoring source.

discovered.name: kguardian-dev/kguardian
discovered.fit_reason: eBPF-based runtime profile generator (NetworkPolicy + seccomp) with an MCP server — fits opsbench's forensic story by letting an incident-response agent auto-propose hardened policies after a compromised-pod investigation.
discovered.notes: eBPF requires privileged DaemonSet — needs special handling in opsbench's per-agent tool allowlist + Cedar policy. Worth a dedicated 'post-incident hardening' skill.

trends: MCP is converging on two layers in mid-2026: distro-agnostic Kubernetes API servers (containers/kubernetes-mcp-server, awslabs/mcp, kubectl-mcp-server crossing the 1k-9k star band) and distro-native gRPC/CLI servers for Talos, k3s, microk8s where API-only access is insufficient for node-level forensics. Kubernetes-native agent runtimes (kagent, Agent Sandbox) are graduating from research to production, pushing a CRD-per-agent + MCP-per-tool pattern. Cluster bootstrap tools (k0sctl, ksail, kubespray) are now shipping AGENTS.md and built-in MCP servers, signalling upstreams are courting LLM consumers directly.
gaps: No one has shipped a forensic-first MCP that treats every mutating call as evidence-sealed-and-signed the way opsbench does — existing servers expose confirm-flags or read-only toggles but no Cedar-style ABAC or SHA-256 chain-of-custody. There is also no unified 'multi-distro incident triage' skill that fans out k3s/Talos/RKE2/EKS-Anywhere checks behind a single agent; everyone ships one server per distro. Finally, the cluster-lifecycle CLIs (k0sctl, kubespray, talosctl, microk8s) lack opinionated, agent-allowlist-shaped wrappers — a clear opening for opsbench team-platform-engineering recipes using CLI-Anything.

trends: Progressive-delivery tooling is rapidly converging on agent-driven GitOps: argoproj-labs and Akuity both shipped first-class Argo CD MCP servers in 2025-2026, and Akuity Intelligence productized an On-Call Agent + Promotion Advisor loop reporting 50-70% faster MTTR. Kargo v1.9 (Mar 2026) brought infrastructure-aware multi-stage promotions including Terraform/OpenTofu steps, and feature-flag platforms (OpenFeature) shipped their own MCP, pointing toward a stack where canary traffic, image promotion, and flag flips are all agent-callable. Verification is consolidating on Prometheus/Datadog metric checks driven through dedicated metric MCPs.
gaps: There is no first-party MCP server for Argo Rollouts itself (the canary/blue-green CRD layer) or for Kargo's promotion API. Both are accessed today only through CLI wrappers or generic Kubernetes MCPs, leaving a clean opening for opsbench to publish argo-rollouts-mcp and kargo-mcp recipes with Cedar-gated promote/abort/pause/setImage tools. Nobody has yet published an evidence-sealed, audit-ready progressive-delivery skill that combines rollout control + PromQL verification + flag flip into one atomic, policy-gated workflow with SHA-256 sealed runbook outputs, which is exactly opsbench's differentiator. Cross-controller (Rollouts + Flagger + Keptn) abstractions for agents are also missing, leaving a blueprint opportunity for a vendor-neutral 'canary verb' interface.

discovered.name: argoproj-labs/mcp-for-argocd
discovered.fit_reason: Official argoproj-labs MCP server exposing Argo CD app/sync/resource tools. opsbench team-platform-engineering recipes can call list_applications, sync_application, get_application_resource_tree, and run_resource_action to drive GitOps progressive-delivery flows under Cedar policy gating.
discovered.notes: TypeScript stdio + HTTP transports; supports read-only mode (good for safe agent default). Argo Rollouts CRDs aren't first-class but reachable via get_application_managed_resources / get_resource_actions to promote/abort rollouts.

discovered.name: akuity/argocd-mcp
discovered.fit_reason: Vendor-backed (Akuity, original Argo creators) MCP server. Mature surface for Argo CD with natural-language to ArgoCD-API translation; opsbench can pin this as the canonical progressive-delivery MCP for AKS-targeted incident-response recipes.
discovered.notes: Production-positioned. Pairs with Akuity Intelligence agents (Promotion Advisor, On-Call Agent), useful prior art for opsbench's per-agent allowlist + evidence-sealing model.

discovered.name: akuity/kargo
discovered.fit_reason: Kargo is the GitOps promotion engine for multi-stage progressive delivery (dev->stage->prod with verification gates). opsbench can wrap the kargo CLI via cli-anything to give team-platform-engineering agents a promote/abort/freeze surface; Kargo's PromotionTask model maps cleanly onto opsbench's evidence-sealed steps.
discovered.notes: v1.9 (Mar 2026) added infra-aware promotions, Terraform steps, GHA dispatch/wait promotion steps. No standalone MCP server yet, wrapping the CLI is the right move and the OpenAPI spec is rich for codegen.

discovered.name: argoproj/argo-rollouts
discovered.fit_reason: Canonical progressive-delivery controller (canary, blue-green, analysis). The kubectl argo rollouts plugin (promote, abort, retry, set image, status) is exactly the surface a team-incident-response skill needs to gate or roll back a bad release; wrap under Cedar policy with the existing kubectl allowlist pattern.
discovered.notes: CNCF graduated. kubectl plugin is stable; metrics-based AnalysisTemplate ties into Prometheus MCP for verification. No first-party MCP server, but the kubectl-argo-rollouts subcommand list is small and ideal for an opsbench skill manifest.

discovered.name: fluxcd/flagger
discovered.fit_reason: Flux's progressive-delivery operator (canary, A/B, blue/green/mirror) with webhook-driven gates. opsbench can build a skill that reads Canary CRD status and posts webhook callbacks during an incident to halt a bad canary; complements Argo Rollouts for Flux-aligned shops.
discovered.notes: Helm chart v1.43.0. Integrates with Istio, Linkerd, Contour, Gloo, NGINX, Skipper, Traefik; metrics from Prometheus, Datadog, NewRelic, CloudWatch. CRD-only surface, easy to wrap via kubectl MCP.

discovered.name: Heapy/argo-workflows-mcp
discovered.fit_reason: MCP server for Argo Workflows (the CI/orchestration sibling to Rollouts). Useful for opsbench when progressive-delivery promotion is driven by Argo Workflows pipelines; permission flags + SQLite-backed audit fit opsbench's evidence-sealing posture.
discovered.notes: HTTP/SSE transport, not stdio, slightly awkward for spawn-per-call agent model. Permission settings in SQLite are a good template for opsbench's per-agent allowlist work.

discovered.name: open-feature/mcp
discovered.fit_reason: OpenFeature MCP server exposes Remote Flag Evaluation (OFREP) plus SDK guidance. Progressive delivery increasingly pairs canary traffic with feature-flag gating; opsbench can let agents flip kill-switches mid-incident via this MCP instead of redeploying.
discovered.notes: Vendor-neutral (CNCF). npx install. Two tools today: install-guidance + flag-eval (single/bulk). Useful blueprint for opsbench's flag-flip remediation step.

discovered.name: argoproj-labs/argocd-image-updater
discovered.fit_reason: Closes the loop on automated container-image promotion into Argo CD (SemVer/digest/newest strategies, Git write-back). opsbench can use the ImageUpdater CR as a pattern reference for how to express constrained, auditable rollout updates from an agent.
discovered.notes: Not a tool-callable surface directly, but the CR schema (update strategy + allowed-tags constraints + Git write-back) is a near-perfect blueprint for an opsbench Cedar-policy-gated 'promote image' agent action.

discovered.name: pab1it0/prometheus-mcp-server
discovered.fit_reason: Canary AnalysisTemplates and Flagger metric checks both depend on PromQL. An agent that drives Argo Rollouts also needs to verify SLOs via Prometheus, this MCP provides execute_query, execute_range_query, list_metrics for that loop.
discovered.notes: Sibling implementations (idanfishman/prometheus-mcp, giantswarm/mcp-prometheus) cover the same surface; pick one canonical version. Read-only auth recommended; ideal companion to any rollout MCP.

discovered.name: keptn/lifecycle-toolkit
discovered.fit_reason: CNCF Keptn provides SLO-driven pre/post-deployment evaluations (quality gates) for progressive delivery. opsbench can mirror Keptn's KeptnApp + KeptnEvaluation CRDs as a pattern for evidence-backed promotion gates without bringing Keptn itself in.
discovered.notes: Cloud-native, GitOps-agnostic. Strong DORA/SLO evaluation primitives. No MCP server upstream; blueprint value is in its declarative pre/post-deployment task contracts.

discovered.name: rohitg00/awesome-claude-code-toolkit
discovered.fit_reason: Ships Claude Code skills explicitly for blue-green / canary / rolling-update strategies, including auto-rollback thresholding. opsbench can fork the deployment-engineer skill manifest and re-target it at Argo Rollouts + Flagger CRDs under its own policy harness.
discovered.notes: Bundle of skills (not single-purpose). Take only the deployment-engineer + kubernetes-specialist subskills; rewrite tool allowlists to match opsbench's Cedar gates.

discovered.name: Akuity Intelligence On-Call Agent + Promotion Advisor
discovered.fit_reason: Closed-source SaaS but its public docs describe an agentic SRE loop (detect -> diagnose -> remediate -> verify) tied to Argo CD/Kargo. Strong reference architecture for opsbench's team-incident-response progressive-delivery recipes, especially around runbook execution + verification gating.
discovered.notes: SaaS; no public source. Use as architecture inspiration only. Public blog 'Beyond Dashboards: AI Agents for GitOps Operations' is the most useful read for opsbench design conversations.

discovered.name: controlplaneio-fluxcd/flux-operator (incl. cmd/mcp MCP server)
discovered.fit_reason: Ships a purpose-built Flux MCP server that lets an agent traverse GitOps state from ResourceSets/HelmReleases/Kustomizations down to pods and logs. Drop-in tool surface for an opsbench team-platform-engineering or team-incident-response skill that needs GitOps-aware remediation while keeping Cedar-policy gating intact.
discovered.notes: AGPL-3.0 limits embedding/vendoring; safe as an external MCP recipe (network-attached tool) rather than vendored code. Excellent prior art for incident-response evidence trails since it diffs cluster state vs Git source.

discovered.name: kagent-dev/kagent
discovered.fit_reason: CNCF Sandbox Kubernetes-native agent framework with Agent/MCPServer/RemoteMCPServer/ModelConfig CRDs, controller reconciliation, OTel traces, and Istio mTLS. Directly maps to opsbench's per-agent allowlist model and provides a credible blueprint for declarative agent deployment with RBAC and admission control.
discovered.notes: Ships its own MCP server bundle for Kubernetes/Istio/Helm/Argo/Prometheus/Grafana/Cilium - those bundles can be cherry-picked as additional MCP recipes. Worth a cross-reference even if opsbench doesn't run on Kagent.

discovered.name: containers/kubernetes-mcp-server
discovered.fit_reason: Most feature-complete Kubernetes+OpenShift MCP server: kubectl/Helm operations, non-destructive mode, secrets masking, OTel tracing, built-in troubleshooting prompt. Slots in as the base K8s tool surface for opsbench team-incident-response, complementing the existing AKS forensic recipe with general kubectl/Helm/CR operations.
discovered.notes: Has secrets-masking and a non-destructive mode that pairs naturally with Cedar policy gating; documented Claude Code getting-started guide already exists. Strong default pick before any vendor-specific operator MCP.

discovered.name: operator-framework/operator-sdk
discovered.fit_reason: Canonical SDK with scaffolding, bundle build/validate, and OLM packaging commands. A team-platform-engineering skill can shell out to operator-sdk for scaffold/build/validate/bundle workflows; cli-anything-wrap is a clean path until an official MCP lands.
discovered.notes: Stable, well-documented, deterministic CLI output - friendly to evidence-sealing. Plugin model already supports Ansible/Helm operators which broadens what an agent can scaffold.

discovered.name: operator-framework/kubectl-operator
discovered.fit_reason: kubectl plugin that is a package manager for OLM operators: install/uninstall/list/upgrade. Exactly the surface a team-incident-response agent needs when responding to a CVE in an installed operator or when team-platform-engineering needs to roll an operator subscription.
discovered.notes: Now also speaks OLMv1 via operator-controller; CLI surface is small and predictable, ideal for wrapping with an allowlist-constrained tool definition.

discovered.name: operator-framework/operator-controller (OLMv1) + catalogd
discovered.fit_reason: OLMv1 central controller plus catalogd FBC server are the modern install/upgrade backbone for cluster extensions. opsbench skills that need to query installed-operator inventory, supported upgrade edges, or catalog content can target the ClusterCatalog/ClusterExtension APIs directly.
discovered.notes: OLMv1 is GA-track in 2026; an opsbench recipe should target the OLMv1 CRDs (ClusterCatalog, ClusterExtension) rather than legacy CSV/Subscription objects.

discovered.name: kubernetes-sigs/mcp-lifecycle-operator
discovered.fit_reason: Declarative Kubernetes operator for deploying/rolling out MCP servers themselves - directly relevant if opsbench wants to run its own MCP server fleet in a cluster with controlled rollout and ecosystem integrations. Strong governance match.
discovered.notes: kubernetes-sigs governance lends credibility; still early (low stars, fast moving). Good template for an opsbench MCP-fleet management blueprint.

discovered.name: rashmigottipati/operator-sdk-mcp-server
discovered.fit_reason: MCP server that exposes operator-sdk scaffolding/build commands to AI assistants. Useful prior art / drop-in for team-platform-engineering tasks that involve generating new operators rather than running them.
discovered.notes: Author affiliation (operator-framework maintainer rashmigottipati) is a quality signal even though star count is low. License unknown - verify before vendoring.

discovered.name: anik120/olmv0-mcp-server
discovered.fit_reason: MCP server for OLMv0 resources (Subscription, CSV, InstallPlan). For clusters still on OLMv0 - including most OpenShift production today - this is the fastest way to give an agent OLM tool surface. Pair with a future OLMv1 MCP.
discovered.notes: Anik Bhattacharjee is an OLM maintainer (quality signal). Slightly stale; verify license. Likely needs companion OLMv1 coverage soon.

discovered.name: vfarcic/crossplane-mcp
discovered.fit_reason: MCP server for Crossplane composite resources (XRDs, Compositions, Claims). Crossplane is the dominant control-plane-as-CRDs project - an opsbench team-platform-engineering skill that issues infra changes via Crossplane needs this surface.
discovered.notes: Maintained by Viktor Farcic (DevOps Toolkit). Useful template even if final opsbench recipe is custom; complements upbound marketplace MCP.

discovered.name: argoproj-labs/mcp-for-argocd
discovered.fit_reason: Official-org MCP server for Argo CD covering Application/AppProject sync, rollback, and status. Pairs with the Flux MCP server so opsbench can support both major GitOps engines from one team-incident-response skill.
discovered.notes: argoproj-labs sub-org gives reasonable governance; sync-driven workflows align well with Cedar gating on destructive operations.

discovered.name: kubernetes-sigs/kubebuilder
discovered.fit_reason: Canonical Kubernetes API/CRD scaffolding framework. A team-platform-engineering skill that generates new controllers should shell out to kubebuilder; relevant issue #5551 (March 2026) is actively adding AI-skill scaffolding to projects, signaling first-class agent support upstream.
discovered.notes: Issue #5551 'Add AI skills in the scaffolds' is the official upstream path - watch for AGENTS.md / .claude/ scaffolds that opsbench can adopt verbatim.

discovered.name: rohitg00/awesome-claude-code-toolkit (kubernetes-specialist agent)
discovered.fit_reason: Pre-written Claude Code subagent for Kubernetes including custom-operator design, CRDs, NetworkPolicies, PDBs. Useful reference for the prompt design of opsbench team-platform-engineering's operator-design agent.
discovered.notes: VoltAgent and jeffallan also publish similar specialists; license varies (check per-repo). Prompt template only - no tool surface.

discovered.name: authzed/controller-idioms
discovered.fit_reason: Generic libraries for idiomatic controllers (state machines, sync handlers). Worth referencing if opsbench ever ships its own operator/controller for managing agent deployments or sealed-evidence storage.
discovered.notes: Pure Go library; not agent-facing. Cross-reference only for any future opsbench-controller component.

trends: In 2026 the operator ecosystem is rapidly converging on MCP as the agent-facing surface: every major control-plane project (Flux, Argo CD, Crossplane, OLM, kagent) now ships or hosts an MCP server, and CNCF Sandbox just admitted kagent which treats Agent/MCPServer/RemoteMCPServer as first-class CRDs. The Operator Framework is splitting along the OLMv0/OLMv1 line - OLMv1 (operator-controller + catalogd + ClusterCatalog/ClusterExtension) is the future surface for any agent that installs or upgrades operators. Upstream Kubebuilder issue #5551 (March 2026) signals scaffolding-level adoption: future operators will ship AGENTS.md/.claude assets out of the box, and "agentic operators" that close the loop between LLM reasoning and GitOps reconciliation are a recognized pattern.
gaps: There is no opsbench-grade incident-response MCP recipe that combines OLM install/upgrade inventory, GitOps drift detection, and Cedar-style policy gating in one tool surface - existing MCPs each cover one slice (Flux, Argo, OLMv0) but none unify them with evidence sealing. OLMv1 also lacks a dedicated MCP server (only the legacy olmv0-mcp-server exists), leaving a clean opportunity for opsbench to publish the first OLMv1-aware ClusterExtension/ClusterCatalog MCP recipe. Finally, no public skill yet wraps operator-sdk + kubebuilder scaffolding with policy-bounded "generate and propose-PR" workflows tailored for platform-engineering teams - opsbench could ship that as a flagship team-platform-engineering skill.

discovered.name: controlplaneio-fluxcd/flux-operator (Flux MCP Server)
discovered.fit_reason: Production-grade MCP server (Go binary, SLSA-3) that lets an opsbench team-incident-response or platform-engineering agent debug GitOps pipelines end-to-end, diff Flux configs across clusters, and trigger reconcile/suspend operations. Has a read-only mode and secret masking that fits opsbench's Cedar-policy + evidence-sealing model.
discovered.notes: AGPL-3.0 means recipes that vendor it must stay open; safer to wire as an external MCP endpoint. Upstream is ControlPlane (commercial backers).

discovered.name: fluxcd/agent-skills
discovered.fit_reason: Three reusable agent skills (gitops-knowledge, gitops-repo-audit, gitops-cluster-debug) already structured for Claude Code's .agents/skills/ layout. Drop-in template for opsbench's team-platform-engineering skill pack covering Flux, Helm, Kustomize manifest generation and repo audit.
discovered.notes: Skills explicitly depend on the Flux MCP server for cluster-debug; pair the two. Multi-agent compatible (Claude Code, Codex).

discovered.name: argoproj-labs/mcp-for-argocd
discovered.fit_reason: Argo-project-blessed MCP server (donated by Akuity engineers) exposing list_applications, sync, rollback, ApplicationSet/AppProject mgmt over stdio+HTTP. Lets an incident-response agent diagnose and remediate failed syncs without writing curl/argocd CLI by hand.
discovered.notes: Officially registered in the MCP Registry; published as npm `argocd-mcp`. Strong fit for forensic incident response (compare deployed vs git-desired state).

discovered.name: hashicorp/terraform-mcp-server
discovered.fit_reason: Vendor-official Terraform MCP server with toolsets for provider/module/policy discovery and HCP Terraform workspace/run management. Critical for a future team-platform-engineering agent that audits or generates IaC during postmortems on infra-drift incidents.
discovered.notes: Doc note: 'intended for local use only' — wire behind opsbench's per-agent tool allowlist; pair with Cedar policy gating for create_run/apply_run.

discovered.name: pulumi/mcp-server
discovered.fit_reason: Vendor-official Pulumi MCP server exposing stack querying, Lucene resource search, policy violations, registry schema lookup, and the Pulumi Neo delegation tool. Gives opsbench cross-IaC coverage alongside Terraform/OpenTofu for multi-stack platform teams.
discovered.notes: GA-positioned but star count modest; Neo delegation tool launches autonomous remediation — must be Cedar-gated in opsbench.

discovered.name: opentofu/opentofu-mcp-server
discovered.fit_reason: Foundation-maintained MCP server (also hosted at mcp.opentofu.org) exposing 5 registry tools for provider/module/resource/datasource lookup. Useful when an opsbench skill needs to generate accurate Tofu/Terraform fragments during a config-drift remediation; hosted variant means no infra to operate.
discovered.notes: Registry-only (no plan/apply); pair with Terraform MCP for execution. Hosted endpoint reduces wiring cost for opsbench recipes.

discovered.name: ansible/vscode-ansible (Ansible Dev Tools MCP)
discovered.fit_reason: Red Hat-maintained Ansible MCP server (Tech Preview in AAP 2.6.4) providing scaffolding, ansible-lint, navigator-based execution, and environment validation. Lets opsbench cover the configuration-management half of DevOps (not just K8s) for hybrid fleets.
discovered.notes: Distributed via npm `@ansible/ansible-mcp-server` and ghcr image; Tech Preview status — flag as experimental in opsbench docs.

discovered.name: zekker6/mcp-helm
discovered.fit_reason: Focused Helm-repo MCP server (list charts, fetch values, parse images, dependency graph) that doesn't need Helm installed locally. Perfect read-only surface for an incident-response agent doing 'what version is running and what changed' diffs without execute privileges.
discovered.notes: Supports OCI registries, basic/mTLS auth. 22 releases — actively maintained by a single author; small attack surface.

discovered.name: rohitg00/kubectl-mcp-server
discovered.fit_reason: 253-tool MCP server covering kubectl + Helm v3 lifecycle + GitOps (Flux/ArgoCD) + cert-manager + Kyverno/Gatekeeper + Velero in one binary. Could replace many opsbench shell-outs in team-incident-response with a single allowlisted MCP — but the breadth requires aggressive per-tool gating.
discovered.notes: Huge tool surface is a double-edged sword for opsbench's per-agent allowlist model; recommend importing only specific tools via Cedar policy.

discovered.name: alexei-led/k8s-mcp-server
discovered.fit_reason: Tightly scoped MCP bridge to kubectl + helm + istioctl + argocd CLIs. Easier to allowlist than the 253-tool variant and aligned with opsbench's existing K8s forensic recipes; can be slotted into team-incident-response with minimal Cedar policy work.
discovered.notes: Includes per-command timeouts and a context allowlist already — design parallels opsbench's allowlist model.

discovered.name: LukasNiessen/kubernetes-skill (KubeShark)
discovered.fit_reason: Failure-mode-first Claude Code skill with reference files for helm-patterns.md and kustomize-patterns.md plus conditional retrieval for Argo CD/Flux. Directly portable as opsbench's team-platform-engineering 'safe-manifest-authoring' skill to reduce LLM hallucinations in incident remediation PRs.
discovered.notes: Skill-format only (no MCP); ideal template-blueprint for opsbench skill authoring conventions.

discovered.name: qwedsazxc78/devops-ai-skill
discovered.fit_reason: Cross-platform (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini) skill pack containing Horus (terraform-validate/security, helm-version-upgrade, helm-scaffold) and Zeus (kustomize-resource-validation, ingress/Gateway-API migrations) — a useful reference for designing opsbench's multi-host skill packs.
discovered.notes: Low stars but well-scoped pipelines (*upgrade, *security, *validate); copy structure rather than vendor.

discovered.name: ahmedasmar/devops-claude-skills
discovered.fit_reason: A Claude Code skills marketplace specifically for DevOps workflows — useful as a discovery surface and pattern reference for opsbench skill metadata, manifest format, and Helm/Kustomize/ArgoCD coverage.
discovered.notes: Marketplace-style repo; primarily a catalog. Use for ideas, not direct vendoring.

discovered.name: njayp/ophis
discovered.fit_reason: Go library that converts any Cobra-based CLI into an MCP server with auto PATH capture — direct fit for opsbench's CLI-Anything-style pattern to wrap kustomize, kapp, ytt, kapitan, or tanka behind MCP without writing per-tool servers.
discovered.notes: Only useful for Cobra CLIs (most CNCF Go tools qualify). Pair with opsbench's per-tool Cedar policies to gate which subcommands the agent can call.

discovered.name: raghavkhokale/gitops-mcp
discovered.fit_reason: 'Config-driven MCP server that turns Claude Code into an approval-gated, read-only-by-default GitOps assistant for any platform team' — directly parallels opsbench's approval-gated philosophy; worth studying for the YAML config schema and policy model.
discovered.notes: Zero stars but the design language (config-driven, approval-gated, read-only default) matches opsbench's Cedar-policy gating; pure pattern reference.

discovered.name: vfarcic/crossplane-mcp
discovered.fit_reason: Early-MVP MCP server for Crossplane claims; useful for future opsbench team-platform-engineering coverage of XRD/Composition/MR workflows. Configurable API group means it can be retargeted to any organization's Crossplane catalog.
discovered.notes: Currently only ListClaims; treat as a blueprint for the surface, not a turnkey integration. Author is a well-known CNCF advocate (Viktor Farcic).

discovered.name: anthropics/skills (official)
discovered.fit_reason: Anthropic's canonical skill collection sets the format and metadata conventions opsbench skills should follow — useful as a structural/format reference (SKILL.md, frontmatter, references/) for any new config-mgmt skill opsbench publishes.
discovered.notes: Use as the canonical schema reference for opsbench skill authoring. Does not itself ship config-mgmt-specific content.

trends: By mid-2026 the config-mgmt MCP space has consolidated around vendor-blessed servers: HashiCorp (Terraform), Pulumi, Red Hat (Ansible), OpenTofu, Argoproj-labs (ArgoCD), and ControlPlane/Flux all ship official or quasi-official MCP servers, and Anthropic merged slash-commands into Skills in Jan 2026 so the skill format is now the lingua franca across Claude Code, Codex, Gemini, and Cursor. The hot pattern is "Skill on top of MCP" (e.g., fluxcd/agent-skills depending on flux-operator MCP, KubeShark grounding Helm/Kustomize patterns) rather than monolithic agents. A second trend is "wide kubectl+helm+gitops MCPs" (kubectl-mcp-server at 253 tools, alexei-led at ~4 CLIs) — convenient but pushing teams to layer policy/allowlist on top.
gaps: There is still no first-class MCP server for KCL, CUE, Jsonnet/Tanka, Kapitan, or the Carvel stack (ytt/kapp/kbld) — only registry-style coverage and skill-level references. Opsbench could ship a 'kustomize-build + diff' MCP (no quality wrapper exists yet) and a 'CUE/KCL evaluate-and-validate' MCP, both of which are widely requested but only available as raw CLIs today. There is also no opinionated config-mgmt skill pack that combines safe Helm deploy, Kustomize overlay, Crossplane claim, and Cedar-policy-gated GitOps rollback into one evidence-sealed workflow — exactly the kind of incident-response narrative opsbench already has the primitives for.

discovered.name: istio/istio
discovered.fit_reason: Ships istioctl which exposes proxy-config, analyze, authn tls-check, experimental wait, and version commands that an opsbench team-incident-response agent shells out to during sidecar/mesh forensics. Wrap with CLI-Anything or wrap-as-skill for read-only diagnostic surface gated by Cedar policy.
discovered.notes: Massive surface area; restrict allowlist to istioctl analyze, proxy-config, proxy-status, ps, x precheck. Avoid install/uninstall paths in agent skills.

discovered.name: cilium/cilium-cli
discovered.fit_reason: Single binary for status, connectivity tests, BGP, Hubble flow taps, policy verdict checks, and mesh diagnostics. Ideal CLI for opsbench team-incident-response when investigating dropped flows or identity mismatches in Cilium-mesh / Cilium Service Mesh deployments.
discovered.notes: Commands like cilium connectivity test mutate cluster state; lock skill to status, hubble observe, policy get, bgp routes, sysdump.

discovered.name: linkerd/linkerd2
discovered.fit_reason: linkerd CLI (check, diagnostics, viz tap/top/stat, edges, identity) covers the same forensic surface opsbench already exposes for kubectl. Direct fit for a linkerd skill in team-incident-response that runs read-only viz and check commands.
discovered.notes: OSS edge releases still active; some advanced features moved to Buoyant Enterprise for Linkerd. Pin to stable channel.

discovered.name: kumahq/kuma
discovered.fit_reason: kumactl exposes inspect, install observability, get/policies dataplanes, and zone diagnostics. Good fit if opsbench needs multi-mesh coverage beyond Istio; suitable as a parallel skill or template-blueprint for non-Istio meshes.
discovered.notes: Smaller install base than Istio/Linkerd; only ship the skill when a customer actually runs Kuma.

discovered.name: hashicorp/consul
discovered.fit_reason: consul CLI plus REST API (catalog, intentions, services, connect, debug) covers HashiCorp service-mesh forensics including intention checks and Envoy sidecar bootstrap inspection. Useful for hybrid VM+K8s shops opsbench targets.
discovered.notes: License changed to BUSL-1.1 in 2023; OK for end-user agent use but blocks any vendoring/redistribution. Cross-reference only, do not embed source.

discovered.name: kiali/kiali
discovered.fit_reason: Kiali exposes a documented REST/GraphQL API for mesh topology graphs, validation messages, workload health, traces and metrics. Perfect upstream for an opsbench MCP recipe that gives the incident-response agent a structured view of Istio service topology and golden signals without shelling out.
discovered.notes: No first-party MCP server yet; opsbench should write a thin MCP shim over /api/namespaces/{ns}/health, /api/namespaces/graph, /api/istio/validations. Auth via kube SA token.

discovered.name: meshery/meshery
discovered.fit_reason: CNCF cloud-native manager covering Istio, Linkerd, Consul, Kuma, Cilium Service Mesh, OSM under one control plane with a REST API and SMI conformance. Good blueprint for a multi-mesh adapter pattern in opsbench platform-engineering team.
discovered.notes: Heavyweight install; copy the adapter abstraction (per-mesh driver interface) rather than running the whole control plane. MeshMate AI assistant is upstream proof that LLM+mesh is viable.

discovered.name: cilium/hubble
discovered.fit_reason: hubble observe with filters (--from-pod, --to-fqdn, --verdict DROPPED, --protocol http) is the canonical L3-L7 flow tap for Cilium mesh forensics. Direct fit for an opsbench skill that captures evidence-sealed flow snapshots during an incident.
discovered.notes: Output is JSON-line friendly for SHA-256 sealing. Requires hubble relay port-forward or in-cluster execution; document the network prereqs in the skill.

discovered.name: cilium/tetragon
discovered.fit_reason: eBPF-based runtime visibility and enforcement that complements service-mesh forensics with process/syscall/network attribution. Pairs with opsbench Cedar-policy gating to gather kernel-level evidence around suspect mesh workloads.
discovered.notes: tetra CLI exists for getevents/policy. Mind privilege requirements; agent skill should only call read paths.

discovered.name: kubeshark/kubeshark
discovered.fit_reason: Wireshark for Kubernetes that captures L4-L7 traffic across pods/sidecars including Istio/Linkerd mTLS-flanked flows once injected. Highly valuable for opsbench team-incident-response to grab pcap-equivalent evidence sealed by SHA-256.
discovered.notes: Tap pods are intrusive (CAP_NET_ADMIN, eBPF). Cedar policy should require explicit approval before kubeshark tap. Self-hosted UI; agent should consume its REST/WS query API.

discovered.name: k8sgpt-ai/k8sgpt
discovered.fit_reason: Ships analyzers and integrations for Istio (VirtualService, Gateway), and supports custom analyzers; opsbench can use k8sgpt's structured analyzer protocol as a tool the agent calls, or vendor the Istio analyzer rules into an opsbench skill.
discovered.notes: Has an MCP server mode in recent releases; could be loaded directly as an MCP server for incident-response. Bring-your-own-LLM friendly.

discovered.name: robusta-dev/holmesgpt
discovered.fit_reason: On-call SRE agent with built-in toolsets for kubectl, Prometheus, Loki, Grafana, Datadog and explicit Istio runbooks. Perfect cross-reference and template for opsbench team-incident-response patterns (toolset YAMLs, runbook prompts, evidence collection).
discovered.notes: Their toolset YAML schema is a good prior art for opsbench tool allowlists. Watch overlap before duplicating effort.

discovered.name: manusa/kubernetes-mcp-server
discovered.fit_reason: Production-quality MCP server for Kubernetes with helm and resource introspection. Service-mesh CRDs (VirtualService, DestinationRule, TrafficPolicy, MeshPolicy, CiliumNetworkPolicy) are reachable via generic resource tools, giving an immediate read-only mesh surface in Claude Code.
discovered.notes: Use as the base MCP server for opsbench's mesh CRD reads; layer mesh-specific MCP tools only where this server is insufficient (e.g., Envoy admin API, Hubble flows).

discovered.name: pab1it0/prometheus-mcp-server
discovered.fit_reason: Istio/Linkerd/Consul all export Prometheus metrics (istio_requests_total, response_code, mTLS state, linkerd_*). A Prometheus MCP server is the lowest-friction way to give the opsbench agent quantitative mesh signal during incident triage.
discovered.notes: Several forks/competing servers exist (grafana/mcp-grafana also covers Prometheus). Pick one; lock down by allowlisting specific PromQL queries to keep evidence reproducible.

discovered.name: solo-io/gloo
discovered.fit_reason: Gloo Edge / Gloo Mesh ship glooctl with check, debug, inspect upstream/route, and istioctl-compatible flows. Worth a thin skill if opsbench targets Solo enterprise mesh shops; otherwise treat as cross-reference.
discovered.notes: Gloo Mesh proper has parts under enterprise license; verify before vendoring anything beyond glooctl.

discovered.name: pixie-io/pixie
discovered.fit_reason: eBPF-based no-instrumentation observability that auto-decodes HTTP/gRPC/mTLS (with key extraction) across service-mesh sidecars. PxL scripts are programmable, making it a strong source for opsbench template scripts the agent could execute during a mesh incident.
discovered.notes: Pace of development slowed since New Relic stewardship change; verify project health before deep integration. PxL scripts (px/service_stats, px/mtls) port well as opsbench prompt templates.

trends: Service-mesh tooling in 2026 is consolidating around two narratives: (1) ambient/sidecarless meshes (Istio Ambient with ztunnel, Cilium Service Mesh on eBPF) are now the default conversation, shifting forensics from "inspect the sidecar" to "inspect identity + eBPF flow"; (2) LLM-driven SRE assistants (k8sgpt, HolmesGPT, MeshMate inside Meshery) have shipped first-class Istio analyzers and toolsets, and HolmesGPT/k8sgpt are racing to expose themselves as MCP servers rather than bespoke chat UIs. The third quiet trend is that mesh vendors still have not shipped first-party MCP servers, so the MCP surface is being built by the K8s/Prometheus ecosystem and consumed transitively.
gaps: There is no maintained MCP server dedicated to a service mesh - no istio-mcp-server, linkerd-mcp-server, hubble-mcp-server, or kiali-mcp-server with stars on GitHub as of June 2026 - which is exactly the surface opsbench is positioned to ship. Specifically missing: a read-only MCP wrapper over istioctl proxy-config / analyze with deterministic JSON output suitable for SHA-256 evidence sealing, an MCP shim over Kiali graph + validation APIs, and an MCP server over hubble observe with Cedar-policy-gated filter scopes. A Cedar-policy library of pre-approved mesh-forensic operations (allow istioctl analyze, deny istioctl install) is also nowhere to be found and would be a differentiator.

discovered.name: grafana/mcp-grafana
discovered.fit_reason: Vendor-published MCP server giving agents tools for dashboards, Prometheus PromQL, Loki LogQL, Tempo trace queries, alerting, incidents, and OnCall. Drops directly into opsbench's team-incident-response as the canonical observability surface during AKS forensic triage.
discovered.notes: Most-used observability MCP in the wild; supports remote HTTP transport (mcp.grafana.com/mcp) for cloud Grafana plus self-host. Needs Cedar policy gating because alert-rule create/update/delete are write tools.

discovered.name: HolmesGPT/holmesgpt
discovered.fit_reason: CNCF Sandbox SRE agent (Robusta + Microsoft) that runs the same agentic loop opsbench is building: pull alerts, query Prometheus/Loki/Grafana/K8s, hypothesize root cause. Ideal reference architecture and toolset registry to port into a team-incident-response skill; its toolset YAML structure maps cleanly to opsbench tool allowlists.
discovered.notes: Has a sister repo robusta-dev/holmes-mcp-integrations exposing Holmes toolsets as MCP. Copying toolset patterns gives opsbench prebuilt evidence-gathering steps without taking on Holmes' runtime dependency.

discovered.name: pab1it0/prometheus-mcp-server
discovered.fit_reason: Lightweight, well-adopted MCP server for raw PromQL queries and metric metadata. Useful when opsbench targets a Prometheus stack without Grafana (bare AKS Managed Prometheus, kube-prometheus-stack) so the incident-response agent can still pull RED/USE metrics during forensic capture.
discovered.notes: Read-only by design — safe to add to a tight tool allowlist. Pairs naturally with ntk148v/alertmanager-mcp-server for the alert side.

discovered.name: SigNoz/signoz-mcp-server
discovered.fit_reason: Vendor MCP for SigNoz (OTel-native APM/logs/traces). Adds an alternative observability backend opsbench skills can target when customers use SigNoz instead of Grafana/Datadog; preserves the same agent-callable surface for distributed-trace evidence in incidents.
discovered.notes: Topics tagged mcp-server/observability/opentelemetry. Active. Good fallback for OTel-first shops.

discovered.name: traceloop/opentelemetry-mcp-server
discovered.fit_reason: Unified MCP that queries OTel traces across Jaeger, Tempo, Traceloop and other backends behind one tool surface. Lets a single opsbench recipe handle distributed-trace forensics regardless of the customer's trace store, reducing per-backend MCP wiring.
discovered.notes: Multi-backend abstraction is rare; verifies it works against Tempo which is the most common AKS pairing. Lower star count but actively maintained.

discovered.name: VictoriaMetrics/mcp-victoriametrics
